steps.title: Identify the appositive and its function
steps.content: A proper name, Marie-Denise Villers, directly renames the noun artist. The key signal: Does this name provide essential information needed to identify the artist, or is it just extra detail?

steps.title: Apply the restrictive appositive rule
steps.content: The name is essential—it uniquely identifies which artist and is the sentence's central point. In English, restrictive appositives (where the identification is essential) use no punctuation. Non-restrictive appositives (where the noun is already identified) use punctuation. The other options use punctuation, incorrectly treating the name as non-essential information. That matches choice B — B is correct.
